commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Claude Web sessionKey renewal tracker, uses renewed keys for later Web requests, persists browser/cache renewals, and adds focused renewal tests.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source and stubs: current main does not observe Set-Cookie renewal, and the PR tests simulate renewed-key reuse; I did not run live Claude or Keychain probes under repository policy.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/live proof and maintainer review of Claude session-cookie persistence; no automated repair is needed now.

target.title: Renew Claude web cookies
target.body:
## Summary
- renew Claude Web session keys from successful `Set-Cookie: sessionKey=...` responses
- reuse a rotated session key for later requests in the same Web fetch
- track renewal for manual cookie fetches too, while keeping manual cookies read-only so they do not rewrite cached browser-derived state

## Root cause
Claude Web usage requests reused a cached `sessionKey` but ignored `Set-Cookie` renewal headers from claude.ai. Once the cached key aged out, later refreshes could keep sending stale credentials. The first patch fixed browser/cache-backed fetches, but the manual-cookie path still skipped the renewal tracker because persistence provenance was also controlling in-memory renewal tracking.

## Fix
- create the renewal tracker for every Claude Web fetch
- use the tracker's current session key for `/usage`, extra usage, and account calls
- keep `CookieHeaderCache.store` gated on `cacheSourceLabel`, so only browser/cache-backed cookies are persisted
- extend the manual-cookie regression test to assert that `/usage` receives the renewed key while the cached browser cookie remains unchanged

## Proof
- `swift test --filter ClaudeWebCookieRenewalTests`
- `make check`
- Redacted live Claude Web fetch on 2026-06-14 17:06 Pacific:
  - browser cookie source found: Safari
  - `/api/organizations` status: 200
  - `/api/organizations/{org}/usage` status: 200
  - cookie cache stored from Safari after the successful fetch
- Redacted live header probe on 2026-06-14 17:06 Pacific:
  - cached session fingerprint: `sha256:e42266eaa620`
  - `/api/organizations` status: 200, `Set-Cookie` headers: 1, `sessionKey` renewal: no
  - `/api/organizations/{org}/usage` status: 200, `Set-Cookie` headers: 1, `sessionKey` renewal: no
  - no real `sessionKey` renewal was emitted by claude.ai during this run, so live renewed-key reuse could not be observed honestly; the renewal/reuse behavior is covered by the request-header regression tests above

Refs #1287
target.author: prospectore
